ml_results.why_best: LogisticRegression was selected on churn-aware validation: churn_recall=0.7971, ahead of the baseline by +0.7971. Weighted F1=0.8006, macro F1=0.7918.
ml_results.preprocessing_notes: Categorical features are imputed and one-hot encoded inside train-fold preprocessing. Numeric features are median-imputed; linear/SVM/KNN models also receive scaling. Identifier columns are removed before this stage. Class imbalance ratio was 1.61; class_weight + train-fold resampling.

explainability_results.plain_english: The top 3 most influential features were Pclass, Sex_female, and Sex_male. Pclass had a mean absolute SHAP value of 0.692, indicating it has the strongest impact on predictions.

global_insights.insight: Pclass is a major driver of model decisions and should be reviewed for business validity.

evaluation_results.recommendation: Churn recall and balanced metrics are acceptable for a first holdout pass; validate on fresh data before deployment.

report_results.executive_summary: InsightForge analyzed Titanic.csv with 891 rows and 10 columns. The workflow identified a classification task and selected LogisticRegression as the strongest model. Churn recall was 0.7971; weighted F1 was 0.8006. Data quality scored 92.62/100 after automated cleaning.

recommendations: Track class-level performance because imbalance was detected and adaptive balancing was used during training.

risk_analysis: Weighted metrics can hide minority-class churn errors.

report_results.deployment_readiness: Ready for offline stakeholder review; not production-ready until threshold policy, monitoring, and fresh holdout validation are completed.
model_limitations: Historical churn labels may encode past retention policies.
Correlation and SHAP explanations are directional diagnostics, not causal proof.
report_results.model_conclusion: Churn recall and balanced metrics are acceptable for a first holdout pass; validate on fresh data before deployment.
